comment.body: An observation:  one point that I understand Alma emphasizes in most posts is for us to look for divergences between the mechanical flows -- what "should happen" based on positioning, etc. -- and what actually happens, the latter revealing the hand of the customer.  Here, we see that RV has been dropping all morning (note the white curve I drew above the RV indicator for emphasis; this is drilled in on a 3min chart).  And even with low local RV, we did not break through the zero vanna line despite repeated tests from above when that low vol should have allowed that to happen.  So if I am understanding her correctly, this confirms her theory.  There is customer buying interest overcoming the mechanics.  Having wrecked some bears with the pop today, perhaps we are basing today to wreck some more bears tomorrow and move into the bull trap phase.  Also, importantly, we are realizing low vol even at these lowish price levels after a nasty drop yesterday, which means increasing liquidity, a primary goal of this market activity before the flush (therefore expect the bull phase to be move up on low vol into positive gamma).  Welcome input or comments on this take.

Welcome input or comments on this take.

comment.body:
do you mean this one? 
//@version=5
indicator("GKYZ Volatility", shorttitle="GKYZ Vol. Est.", overlay=false)



lookback = input.int(5, title="Lookback Period", minval=1)  
ma_period=input.int(20,title="MA Period", minval=1)


f_calcDailyRV() =>



    window          = lookback
    trading_periods = 390



    log_hoD = math.log(high / open)
    log_loD = math.log(low  / open)
    log_coD = math.log(close / open)



    log_ocD    = math.log(open / nz(close[1]))
    log_oc_sqD = log_ocD * log_ocD



    log_ccD    = math.log(close / nz(close[1]))
    log_cc_sqD = log_ccD * log_ccD



    rsD = log_hoD*(log_hoD - log_coD) + log_loD*(log_loD - log_coD)



    close_volD = math.sum(log_cc_sqD, window) / (window - 1)
    open_volD  = math.sum(log_oc_sqD, window) / (window - 1)
    window_rsD = math.sum(rsD, window)        / (window - 1)
    kD         = 0.34 / (1.34 + (window + 1.0)/(window - 1.0))
    rvD        = math.sqrt((open_volD + kD*close_volD + (1 - kD)*window_rsD) * trading_periods)



dailyRV = request.security(syminfo.tickerid, "1", f_calcDailyRV())
ma=ta.sma(dailyRV,ma_period)
plot(dailyRV, title="GKYZ Volatility", color=#970ef3, linewidth=2)
plot(ma,title="Moving Average", color = #00ff00, linewidth=2)

comment.body:
All these OD charts i think are proving the point of a bear trap
- No one is buying OTM puts which is why we see the vix so low
- They are shorting this via ITM puts, which is why we see all that overhead negative vanna.

So there isn't a volatility hedge
